title: Nanoleaf bets its future on robots, red light therapy, and AI
summary: Nanoleaf, traditionally a smart lighting company, is undergoing a strategic "brand evolution" focused on wellness, robotics, and AI to remain competitive as the smart home market saturates. The company has been relatively quiet for two years to develop this new direction, signaling a major shift in its product strategy.
content:
Nanoleaf teased a trio of new products focused on embodied AI as it looks to move its brand beyond smart lighting. | Image: Nanoleaf	

Smart lighting company Nanoleaf has been unusually quiet recently. While competitors such as Govee and Philips Hue have been pumping out new products and innovative features at an impressive pace, Nanoleaf has launched just a handful of smart lighting products in the last two years. There's a reason for this lull - the company has been going through a "brand evolution" focused on wellness, robotics, and, of course, AI. 
"The smart home is getting kind of boring," says the ever-candid Gimmy Chu, CEO and cofounder of Nanoleaf, which he now doesn't want me to call a smart lighting company. "Our brand needs to evolve to incorporate some of the  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Nanoleaf押注未来：机器人、红光疗法和人工智能
zh.summary: 传统智能照明公司Nanoleaf正在进行战略性"品牌演变"，专注于健康、机器人和人工智能，以在智能家居市场饱和时保持竞争力。该公司在过去两年相对沉寂，以开发这个新方向，标志着其产品战略的重大转变。

title: Apple’s education discount now requires proof that you’re a student
summary: Apple now requires US students, teachers, and parents to verify their eligibility through third-party service Unidays when purchasing products at education discount rates online. The policy expansion also marks the first time Apple Watch models qualify for education pricing and is rolling out to Canada, Australia, Hong Kong, and other countries.
content:
Watch models like the Apple Watch Series 11 (pictured) are also now available at discounted education rates for the first time. | Photo by Amelia Holowaty Krales / The Verge	

Apple now requires proof of eligibility from US customers who purchase products online at discounted education rates, which also apply to the Apple Watch for the first time. According to a new pop-up message displayed on Apple's education webstore, Apple is directing students, parents, and teachers to verify their status via Unidays' third-party verification service - a system the Cupertino company has already implemented in other countries, including the UK.
Apple Insider reports that the Unidays service is now also being rolled out to Apple customers in Canada, Australia, Hong Kong, Turkey, and Chile. Eligible customers can use an email a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Apple的教育折扣现在需要学生身份证明
zh.summary: Apple现在要求美国学生、教师和家长在网上购买教育折扣产品时，通过第三方服务Unidays验证其资格。政策扩展还标志着Apple Watch型号首次符合教育定价资格，目前正在加拿大、澳大利亚、香港等国家推出。

title: Everyone’s a loser in Strait of Hormuz game that simulates global crisis
summary: A crisis simulation game challenges players to navigate difficult decisions regarding the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint where competing interests create no clear winning solution. The game illustrates the complexity and inevitable trade-offs inherent in geopolitical conflicts.
content: The game asks players to find the least worst options for a shipping chokepoint.

zh.title: 霍尔木兹海峡游戏中人人都是失败者，模拟全球危机
zh.summary: 一款危机模拟游戏挑战玩家就霍尔木兹海峡做出艰难决定，该海峡是全球航运咽喉，竞争利益导致没有明确的胜利方案。该游戏说明了地缘政治冲突中固有的复杂性和不可避免的权衡。

title: Rocket Report: Alpha Block 2 coming this summer; Falcon sets booster landing mark
summary: SpaceX's Falcon rocket achieved a new booster landing record while Alpha Block 2 is scheduled to launch this summer, marking progress in the company's rocket development priorities. The milestones reflect SpaceX's focus on maximizing team impact for humanity.
content: "The deciding factor was what we felt like was the team’s impact to humanity."

zh.title: 火箭报告：Alpha Block 2 今年夏天推出；猎鹰创造助推器着陆记录
zh.summary: SpaceX猎鹰火箭创造了新的助推器着陆记录，Alpha Block 2计划于今年夏天发射，标志着SpaceX火箭开发的进展。这些里程碑反映了SpaceX专注于为人类最大化团队影响力的目标。

title: Sony’s PS5 sales plummet amid price rises and a memory crisis
summary: Sony's PS5 console sales dropped 46 percent year-over-year to 1.5 million units in the latest quarter, following price increases that raised the console cost from $499.99 to $649.99. The company attributed the decline to global economic pressures and an ongoing memory crisis, now forecasting a 6 percent drop in annual gaming revenue.
content:
Sony sold just 1.5 million PS5 consoles in its most recent fourth fiscal quarter, down 46 percent year over year. The slump in PS5 sales comes after Sony raised the price of its PS5 consoles twice over the past year, pushing the price of the regular PS5 from $499.99 all the way up to $649.99.
Sony blamed "continued pressures in the global economic landscape," for the price hikes in March, amid an ongoing memory crisis and pressure from the war in Iran. Sony now forecasts that annual gaming revenue will drop 6 percent, but these forecasts could be impacted by ongoing memory costs. "We plan to base our PS5 hardware sales in FY26 on the volume …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 索尼PS5销售在价格上涨和内存危机中暴跌
zh.summary: 索尼PS5游戏机销量在上一季度同比下降46%至150万台，主要原因是价格上涨导致主机价格从499.99美元提高到649.99美元。公司将销售下滑归因于全球经济压力和持续的芯片短缺问题，预计今年游戏收入将下降6%。

title: Nintendo is raising Switch 2 prices
summary: Nintendo is raising Switch 2 prices globally effective September 1st, with the US price increasing from $449.99 to $499.99 due to changing market conditions. The price hikes extend internationally with similar percentage increases, and Nintendo is forecasting lower sales for the upcoming year.
content:
Nintendo is raising the price of its Switch 2 console globally, "in light of changes in market conditions," and is now forecasting a drop in sales over the next year. Starting September 1st, the Switch 2 will cost $499.99 in the US, up from its current $449.99 price. 
At the same time, prices will also increase by $50 in Canada ($679.99, up from $629.99) and €40 in Europe, bringing it to €499.99 (about $587). The price increases in Japan go into effect sooner, on May 25th, and impact a greater range of Switch products. The Switch 2 will increase from ¥49,980 (about $318) to ¥59,980 (about $382), with similar increases being applied to the o …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 任天堂提高Switch 2价格
zh.summary: 任天堂从9月1日起全球提高Switch 2价格，美国价格从449.99美元上升到499.99美元，原因是市场条件变化。价格上调也适用于国际市场，幅度相似，任天堂预计来年销售将下降。

title: Why you can never get your doctor to call you back
summary: Basata is an AI company automating medical administrative tasks to improve workflow efficiency. The article examines the tension between augmenting worker productivity and potentially displacing jobs, though current administrative staff are more focused on managing overwhelming workloads.
content: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. For now, the founders say the administrative staff they work with aren't worried about that; they're more worried about drowning.

zh.title: 为什么你永远无法让医生回电话
zh.summary: Basata是一家自动化医疗行政任务以提高工作流效率的人工智能公司。该文章探讨了增强员工生产力与可能的岗位替代之间的矛盾,尽管当前的行政人员更专注于管理压倒性的工作负荷。
